hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3 and reduce off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong ddG but insufficient selectivity. The next iteration should focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to improve Δmargin and achieve SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Off-target binding is likely driven by conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions will allow us to design mutations that disrupt off-target contacts while preserving key pharmacophore residues.

Introducing charged or hydrophilic residues at positions 1 and 2 can disrupt off-target interactions, while smaller hydrophobic residues at positions 5, 6, 11, and 12 can reduce off-target binding without affecting the pharmacophore.
